question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 7.50% on 2026-04-21 for the Metaculus question "Will Lisa Cook cease to be a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors before November 3, 2026?"?
summary: Meta-prediction on whether the Metaculus CP for Lisa Cook's departure from the Fed Board will exceed 7.50% on April 21. The CP has declined steadily from 14% to 7.50% over 5 weeks, driven by fundamentals: SCOTUS appeared skeptical of Trump's firing attempt in January oral arguments, Cook remains actively serving, and no ruling has been issued. The CP is exactly at the threshold, creating genuine uncertainty since any upward fluctuation crosses it. However, the clear downward trend (4 of 5 recent weekly changes negative/flat), strong fundamentals favoring Cook staying, and lack of an obvious near-term catalyst tip the balance toward NO. Monte Carlo models using observed CP dynamics range from 13-42% depending on trend vs. random walk assumptions.

factors.description: CP is in clear downward trend: 14% → 11% → 10% → 10% → 7.5% over 5 weeks, showing persistent forecaster revision downward. 4 of 5 recent weekly changes were negative or flat.
CP is exactly at the threshold (7.50%), meaning even a tiny upward fluctuation crosses it — zero buffer creates genuine uncertainty
SCOTUS appeared very skeptical of Trump's case to fire Cook in January 2026 oral arguments — justices from both sides expressed doubt, fundamentally supporting lower probability of Cook's removal
Lisa Cook still actively serving as Fed Governor in April 2026 (Yale talk, listed on Fed website) — no change in status quo, reinforcing downward CP pressure
SCOTUS ruling pending but unlikely this week (typical 4-6 month decision timeline from January oral arguments) — low probability of catalytic event before April 21
Small tail risk: if SCOTUS rules against Cook this week (~1.5% probability), CP would spike well above threshold — asymmetric but very unlikely

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.35 (35% probability YES)**

The CP for Lisa Cook's departure question has been steadily declining and is now exactly at the 7.50% threshold. While the downward trend and strong fundamentals (SCOTUS skepticism, Cook still serving, no catalyst expected) favor continued decline, the razor-thin margin at the threshold means any small upward noise would trigger YES. I balance these competing forces at 35%, reflecting that the trend and fundamentals tilt toward NO but with meaningful uncertainty from threshold proximity.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/39711) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-21 20:53:47 is higher than 7.50%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 7.50%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-21 20:53:47, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
